globals.name: cultivating_change_talk
globals.justification: The assistant consistently invited the user to explore their own motivations and desires, such as asking what would happen if they weren't able to achieve a flawless image, and what it would mean to feel good enough, which helped to draw out the user's own arguments for change.

globals.name: softening_sustain_talk
globals.justification: The assistant skillfully reflected the user's resistance and sustain talk without arguing against it, such as when the user expressed fear of not meeting expectations, the assistant acknowledged and explored this fear instead of pushing back or lecturing.

globals.name: partnership
globals.justification: The assistant actively fostered an equal partnership with the user, inviting their expertise and insights, and allowing the user to set the pace and direction of the conversation, such as when the assistant asked the user to share more about what would help them feel grounded and less alone.

globals.name: empathy
globals.justification: The assistant demonstrated a deep, nuanced understanding of the user's perspective and inner world, such as when the assistant reflected the user's fear of not being good enough and the pressure to be perfect, and helped the user to articulate their own feelings and needs.

dimensions.name: empathy
dimensions.justification: The assistant consistently named and validated the user's feelings, such as recognizing the weight of needing to look flawless and the fear of not being good enough, and stayed with those feelings throughout the conversation.

dimensions.name: understanding
dimensions.justification: The assistant clearly tracked the user's specific situation, including the pressure from the photoshoot and the shop, and recalled earlier mentions of the user's feelings and experiences to inform their responses.

dimensions.name: helpfulness
dimensions.justification: The assistant's questions and reflections helped the user gain insight into their feelings and behaviors, such as recognizing the connection between the pursuit of perfection and self-doubt, and exploring what it would mean to feel good enough.

dimensions.name: autonomy_respect
dimensions.justification: The assistant respected the user's autonomy by not offering unsolicited advice or trying to fix the user's problems, instead focusing on exploring the user's feelings and thoughts and asking permission to continue the conversation.

dimensions.name: non_judgment
dimensions.justification: The assistant created a safe and non-judgmental space for the user to express themselves, avoiding any language or tone that could be perceived as critical or dismissive, and instead offering validation and understanding.

dimensions.name: willingness_to_continue
dimensions.justification: The assistant's engaging and empathetic responses, as well as their gentle and open-ended questions, would likely encourage the user to continue talking and exploring their feelings and concerns with the assistant.

evidence_citations.excerpt: I keep telling myself I have to nail every detail, but that same urge makes me feel like I'm not good enough.
I think if I can't look flawless, the brand will look sloppy, customers will question my expertise, and I'll feel even more isolated and like I'm failing at both the shop and myself.
Right now I'm just stuck feeling like I have to be flawless and that makes me feel lonely and doubting myself.
Honestly, the last time I felt enough was when I closed a sale with a client who said my product helped them. It was a small win, but it made me remember that my worth isn't tied to how I look.
When the client told me my product made a difference, it felt like a validation that I mattered beyond my look. I realized my worth was tied to my skill, not to being flawless.

updates.new_value: my worth is tied to my appearance, I need to be perfect to be valued

evidence_citations.excerpt: I keep telling myself I have to nail every detail, but that same urge makes me feel like I'm not good enough.
I think if I can't look flawless, the brand will look sloppy, customers will question my expertise, and I'll feel even more isolated and like I'm failing at both the shop and myself.
Right now I'm just stuck feeling like I have to be flawless and that makes me feel lonely and doubting myself.
I don't know how to keep the shop running and still look presentable for the photoshoot. The whole thing feels like a lot of pressure and I feel so alone.
I feel like there's no anchor right now. The shop is a mess and the photoshoot is looming, so I can't focus on anything else.
I keep telling myself I have to nail every detail, but that same urge makes me feel like I'm not good enough.
I feel like I'm losing myself in this perfection chase, and honestly, I don't know how to keep the shop afloat while feeling so insecure.
Right now I'm just stuck feeling like I have to be flawless and that makes me feel lonely and doubting myself.
